rounds.name: Online Assessment
rounds.description: 2 coding problems (1 Medium, 1 Hard) + 2 work simulation questions. 90 minutes total.

problems.title: Optimal Utilization
problems.description: Given two lists of pairs [id, value], find pairs (one from each list) whose values sum is closest to a target.

problems.title: Min Cost to Connect All Nodes
problems.description: Given n nodes and a list of edges with costs, find the minimum cost to connect all nodes. Some nodes may already be connected.

tips: Run all test cases before submitting
Work simulation: choose answers that show Customer Obsession and Ownership
Time management: spend max 40 min per coding problem

rounds.name: Phone Screen
rounds.description: 1 coding problem (Medium) + 2 LP behavioral questions. Shared IDE.

problems.title: LRU Cache
problems.description: Design and implement a data structure for Least Recently Used cache.

behavioral_questions: Tell me about a time you took ownership beyond your role (Ownership LP)
Describe a time you simplified a complex process (Invent and Simplify LP)
tips: Think out loud — the interviewer wants to see your thought process
Ask clarifying questions before coding
Leave 15 min for behavioral questions

rounds.name: Virtual Onsite — Coding
rounds.description: 2 Medium/Hard problems in 55 minutes. Focus on System Design at scale.

problems.title: Meeting Rooms II
problems.description: Given meeting time intervals, find the minimum number of conference rooms required.

problems.title: Word Ladder
problems.description: Transform one word into another by changing one letter at a time.

tips: Optimize for time complexity — Amazon cares about scale
Write clean, production-quality code
Handle edge cases explicitly

rounds.name: Bar Raiser
rounds.description: Deep behavioral dive (3 LPs) + system design component. This interviewer has veto power.
behavioral_questions: Tell me about your most impactful project (Deliver Results)
Describe a time you disagreed with your manager (Have Backbone, Disagree and Commit)
Tell me about a time you made a mistake. How did you fix it? (Earn Trust)
rounds.system_design_topic: Design a URL shortener like bit.ly
tips: Bar Raiser evaluates culture fit — be authentic
Show numbers and metrics in every STAR answer
For system design: start with requirements, not solutions

rounds.name: Phone Screen
rounds.description: 1-2 coding problems on Google Docs/shared IDE. Focus on clean code and optimal solutions.

problems.title: License Key Formatting
problems.description: Given a license key string, reformat it so groups have exactly K characters.

problems.title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
problems.description: Find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.

tips: Google uses Google Docs — practice coding without autocomplete
Talk through your approach before coding
Consider multiple approaches and discuss tradeoffs

rounds.name: Onsite — Coding 1
rounds.description: Algorithm-heavy. Expect graph/tree problems.

problems.title: Number of Islands
problems.description: Given a 2D grid of '1's and '0's, count the number of islands.

problems.title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
problems.description: Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary tree.

tips: Optimal time complexity is expected — brute force won't pass
Write helper functions for cleaner code
Test with edge cases verbally

rounds.name: Onsite — Coding 2
rounds.description: Dynamic programming and advanced data structures.

problems.title: Coin Change
problems.description: Find the minimum number of coins to make a given amount.

problems.title: Design HashMap
problems.description: Design a HashMap without using built-in hash table libraries.

tips: Explain your DP recurrence relation clearly
Time complexity analysis is mandatory
Ask about constraints: input size determines approach

rounds.name: Onsite — System Design
rounds.description: For L3, this might be a lighter design question or a coding design question.
tips: Start with functional requirements
Discuss scale: how many pages per day?
Cover: URL frontier, politeness, deduplication
Draw diagrams — Google interviewers love visual communication

rounds.name: Onsite — Googliness/Behavioral
rounds.description: Full behavioral round. Tests culture fit, collaboration, intellectual humility.
behavioral_questions: Tell me about a time you failed and what you learned
Describe a time you dealt with ambiguity
Tell me about a time you had to influence someone without authority
Describe a situation where you had to disagree and commit
Tell me about your proudest technical accomplishment
tips: Google values humility — admit mistakes genuinely
Show curiosity and love of learning
Don't be arrogant about your achievements

rounds.name: Online Coding Test
rounds.description: 3 coding problems on HackerEarth/CodeNation platform.

problems.title: Sorted Rotated Array Search
problems.description: Search for a target in a rotated sorted array.

problems.title: Minimum Operations to Reduce to Zero
problems.description: Find minimum operations to reduce array sum to zero.

problems.title: Largest Rectangle in Histogram
problems.description: Find the area of the largest rectangle in a histogram.

tips: Flipkart problems are LeetCode Medium-Hard level
Partial scoring — submit even incomplete solutions
Time complexity matters — O(n²) often TLEs

rounds.name: Machine Coding Round
rounds.description: Implement a working system in 90 minutes. Real IDE, runnable code expected.
rounds.system_design_topic: Design a Parking Lot System — implement classes for ParkingLot, Vehicle, Ticket, PaymentProcessor with proper OOP and SOLID principles.
tips: Focus on clean OOP design — use interfaces and abstract classes
Must compile and run — they may test your code
Follow SOLID principles — Single Responsibility is key
Have a working demo ready in 60 min, polish in remaining 30

rounds.name: Problem Solving + DS/Algo
rounds.description: 2 coding problems. Whiteboard/shared IDE. Interviewer watches you code live.

problems.title: Clone Graph
problems.description: Deep copy a graph with random pointers.

problems.title: Merge K Sorted Lists
problems.description: Merge k sorted linked lists into one sorted list.

tips: Think out loud — silent coding is a red flag at Flipkart
Discuss time and space complexity explicitly
Handle edge cases before being asked

rounds.name: Hiring Manager Round
rounds.description: Culture fit + past project deep dive.
behavioral_questions: Tell me about a product you built from scratch
Describe a time you debugged a critical production issue
What's the most complex system you've worked on?
Why Flipkart and not other companies?
tips: Flipkart values builder DNA — show you can build, not just code
Know Flipkart's tech stack (Java, Kafka, MySQL, Kubernetes)
Ask thoughtful questions about their architecture

rounds.name: TCS NQT (National Qualifier Test)
rounds.description: Part A: Aptitude (60 min) — Quant, Logical, Verbal, English. Part B: Coding (60-90 min) — 2 coding problems in C/C++/Java/Python.

problems.title: Palindrome Check with Modifications
problems.description: Check if a string can become palindrome by removing at most one character.

problems.title: Find the Shortest Path in a Grid
problems.description: In a grid with obstacles, find shortest path from top-left to bottom-right.

tips: Aptitude section is elimination — accuracy over speed
Coding: focus on correctness first, optimization second
TCS NQT score determines which track you're placed in (Ninja vs Digital)
Digital cutoff is typically higher — aim for 80%+ in aptitude

rounds.name: Technical Interview
rounds.description: CS fundamentals — DBMS, OS, OOP, CN. 1-2 code-on-paper questions.
behavioral_questions: Explain polymorphism with a real-world example
What is normalization? Explain 1NF, 2NF, 3NF
Write SQL to find the 2nd highest salary
Explain the difference between TCP and UDP
What is deadlock? How can it be prevented?
tips: Brush up on CS fundamentals — they ask textbook questions
Be able to write SQL queries on paper
Know your resume projects inside out — they'll ask about them
Stay calm — TCS interviewers are usually friendly

rounds.name: Managerial + HR Round
rounds.description: Behavioral questions about teamwork, adaptability, and why TCS.
behavioral_questions: Tell me about your biggest college project
Why TCS specifically?
Are you willing to relocate?
Where do you see yourself in 5 years?
Tell me about a time you worked under pressure
tips: Research TCS's recent projects and initiatives
Express willingness to relocate and work flexible hours
Show enthusiasm for learning and growing within TCS
Keep answers concise — 2 minutes max per question

rounds.name: Online Assessment
rounds.description: 2-3 coding problems. Platform: HackerEarth. Focus on problem-solving speed.

problems.title: Valid Parentheses with Wildcards
problems.description: Given a string with '(', ')', and '*', determine if it's valid.

problems.title: Maximum Sum Subsequence No Two Adjacent
problems.description: Find the maximum sum of a subsequence where no two elements are adjacent.

tips: Razorpay values clean, readable code
Handle edge cases — fintech can't afford bugs
Focus on correctness over speed

rounds.name: DSA Round 1
rounds.description: 1 Medium + 1 Hard problem. Shared IDE. Heavy focus on trees, graphs, DP.

problems.title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
problems.description: Find the maximum path sum in a binary tree. Path can start and end at any node.

problems.title: Implement LFU Cache
problems.description: Design a Least Frequently Used cache with O(1) operations.

tips: Razorpay asks harder problems than average — prepare LeetCode Hard
Write modular code — they review code quality
Discuss space-time tradeoffs thoroughly

rounds.name: DSA Round 2 + System Design
rounds.description: 1 coding problem + 1 system design discussion.

problems.title: Rate Limiter
problems.description: Implement a rate limiter that allows N requests per T seconds.

rounds.system_design_topic: Design a payment processing system — handle idempotency, retries, and double-spend prevention.
tips: Fintech system design: focus on consistency, idempotency, audit trails
Razorpay cares deeply about reliability — discuss failure modes
Know about distributed transactions and eventual consistency

rounds.name: Culture Fit + Hiring Manager
rounds.description: Deep dive into past work + culture alignment.
behavioral_questions: What's the hardest bug you've ever fixed?
Tell me about a time you built something you're proud of
How do you handle conflicting priorities?
What do you know about Razorpay's products?
tips: Know Razorpay's products: Payment Gateway, RazorpayX, Payroll
Show passion for fintech and building reliable systems
Ask about their engineering culture and challenges
